body.title: [sprint] Inventory: grep ~/ for dev-process artifacts living outside devlab/runtime/memory/
body.description:
**Problem:** Dev-process artifacts (slates, tickets, goals, sessions, projects, proofs, rules, decisions, notes) may still live in paths other than the one canonical substrate devlab/runtime/memory/. CLAUDE.md mandates one home, no exceptions; a renamed store with surviving write-paths silently splits the source of truth. Before migrating we need the worklist.
**Proposed shape:** Run the Akien-specified sweep across ~/ : for each artifact type keyword (slate, ticket, goal, session, project, proof, rule, decision, note, design_pattern, architecture) cross filename/content with md / json / txt; scope to ~/ . **WHAT MOVES vs WHAT STAYS (critical — encode this, don't rediscover it mid-migration):** only canonical dev-process artifact TYPES migrate. EXEMPT-BY-DEFAULT (these match keywords but are NOT UU dev artifacts — they must NOT migrate): (1) uu_home() runtime state — all of ~/.unseen_university/ (logs, flags, device cachedstate, vault, palace_echo); CLAUDE.md: uu_home is 'not the repo'. (2) The harness's own store ~/.claude/ (its MEMORY.md + project_*/feedback_*/session files — a separate product). (3) Akien's personal area ~/.unseen_university/akien*. (4) .git/ anywhere. (5) /home/akien/.TheIgors/certs (Akien-flagged). (6) Any credential-shaped file (no-keys-in-repo rule — the sweep pulls arbitrary ~/ files toward a git-tracked dir). This is the artifact-vs-runtime-state line CLAUDE.md draws between devlab/runtime/memory/ and uu_home(). Produce a categorized worklist: path -> artifact-type -> in-canonical-store? -> proposed action (migrate / exempt-with-reason / already-canonical). Write the inventory to devlab/runtime/memory/notes/.
**Affected files:** devlab/runtime/memory/notes/substrate-artifact-inventory.YYYYMMDD.md (output only)
**Design rules:** Canonical dev-process memory — one home (CLAUDE.md); artifact-types move, uu_home()/~/.claude/credentials runtime state STAYS; delete old copies only AFTER verified migration (separate ticket); check with Akien before touching .TheIgors/certs.
**Cross-ref:** Coordinate with the OPEN T-theigors-archive-artifact-freeze — it already governs the TheIgors archive dirs under ~/. The inventory must NOT re-propose moving anything that freeze-ticket covers; tag those paths 'governed by T-theigors-archive-artifact-freeze' rather than 'migrate'.
**Scope boundary:** Discovery + worklist ONLY. Do not move, delete, or migrate anything in this ticket.
**Test plan:** The inventory note exists and lists every artifact-bearing path under ~/ outside devlab/runtime/memory/ (minus excludes), each tagged with a proposed action. This inventory IS the baseline measurement for the consolidation hypothesis.

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] Canonical dev-process memory — one home, no exceptions. Every dev-process artifact — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
…and 39 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set

**Provenance / method (Akien, 2026-06-27):** This grep-by-artifact-type approach REPLACES an abandoned folder-by-folder enumeration — Akien started listing folders to review, the list exploded, so he switched to grepping for the artifact TYPES directly and found all the artifacts that way. So: do NOT try to enumerate directories first (that path dead-ends); sweep by type. Use `rg` (ripgrep 14.1.0 is installed) — faster than grep -r and respects .gitignore, with explicit -uu / --no-ignore when you need to reach dotfiles and ignored paths under ~/. Honor the exempt-by-default set above.
